compiled_state.title: [GREEN] Implement purge-bridge subcommand in ticket CLI dispatcher
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Implement the `purge-bridge` subcommand in the ticket CLI dispatcher. This is a safety-critical destructive operation requiring a new script with mount/writable guards, per-ID dry-run output, and audit logging.

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-purge-bridge.sh (new — thin safety layer over purge-non-project-tickets.sh logic with required guards)
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket (modify — add purge-bridge case + update _usage())
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh (modify — add ticket_purge_bridge() function)

## Acceptance Criteria

### Safety guards (MUST implement):
- [ ] ticket-purge-bridge.sh checks tracker directory EXISTS before any deletion (exit 1 with clear error if not)
- [ ] ticket-purge-bridge.sh checks tracker directory is WRITABLE before any deletion (exit 1 with clear error if not writable — both conditions required; false negative permits destructive ops)

### Flags:
- [ ] Accepts: `--keep=<PROJECT_KEY>` (required), `--dry-run` (optional)
- [ ] --dry-run mode lists each individual ticket ID that would be deleted (one per line) to stdout; does NOT delete
- [ ] Without --dry-run: logs each deleted ticket ID to stderr as audit record (`AUDIT: deleted <id>`) before/after deletion

### Dispatcher routing:
- [ ] `ticket_purge_bridge()` added to ticket-lib-api.sh: `bash "$_TICKETLIB_DIR/ticket-purge-bridge.sh" "$@"; return $?`
- [ ] `purge-bridge)` case added to dispatcher after `quality-check)` case: `_ensure_initialized && source ticket-lib-api.sh && _ticketlib_dispatch ticket_purge_bridge "$@" && exit $?`
- [ ] _usage() string updated: "purge-bridge Remove non-project inbound bridge tickets (--keep=<key> [--dry-run])"
- [ ] SOURCEABILITY CONTRACT preserved; _TICKETLIB_DIR used for path

### Scope:
- [ ] Only deletes jira-* ticket directories whose project key does NOT match --keep; never touches non-jira-* tickets
- [ ] After deletion, commits removal on tickets branch (same as purge-non-project-tickets.sh)
- [ ] No Jira API calls; LOCAL-ONLY operation
- [ ] purge-non-project-tickets.sh is NOT modified (scope boundary)
- [ ] Task 9dd3-88ef RED tests turn GREEN after implementation

## Notes
ticket-purge-bridge.sh can reuse the core scanning logic from purge-non-project-tickets.sh (or delegate to it for the actual deletion after running its own safety checks). The TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR env var override must be supported for testability. Use `test -w "$TRACKER_DIR"` for writable check. Executable bit must be set: `chmod +x plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-purge-bridge.sh`.
